text.title: rust-http-handlers Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for rust-http-handlers as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Placing Json<T> before State or Path parameters.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Does not fail at compile time (just extractor ordering) but produces confusing behavior when State is added later and causes trait bound errors.

failure-mode.description: Putting a raw DbPool or Config struct directly in AppState without Arc.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Forces a full clone of potentially large structs per request. Use Arc<DbPool> and Arc<Config> instead.

failure-mode.description: Adding 'a lifetime parameters to handler signatures to "fix" &str params.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lifetime params do not satisfy Axum's Into<Router> bound. The correct fix is to use String (owned) or 'static &str constants.

failure-mode.description: Placing sqlx::query! without DATABASE_URL or .sqlx/ offline files in CI.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: sqlx queries are verified at compile time; CI builds without DATABASE_URL set will fail. Use SQLX_OFFLINE=true and check in .sqlx/.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Holding a std::sync::MutexGuard across .await deadlocks the Tokio runtime. Use tokio::sync::Mutex or prefer message-passing.

failure-mode.description: unwrap() and expect() in prod paths because they look harmless to LLMs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Block via clippy -D unwrap_used; panics crash the Tokio task.

failure-mode.description: &str as a handler param doesn't compile (no 'static lifetime); agent's first fix is often wrong ('a lifetime).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force String; the agent's lifetime fix won't satisfy Axum's Handler trait.

failure-mode.description: Extractor order errors produce confusing trait-bound messages; agent loops on compile errors.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hint that body extractors must come last.

failure-mode.description: sqlx::query! requires DATABASE_URL at compile time or .sqlx/ offline files. Agents miss the offline step in CI.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening

failure-mode.description: tracing::info! without target = overflows logs in hot paths.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening

failure-mode.description: Async + std::sync::Mutex creates deadlocks. Agents reach for sync::Mutex in async code.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use tokio::sync::Mutex (or RwLock) only when needed; prefer message-passing.

failure-mode.description: Any new dep in Cargo.toml, unsafe block, or tokio::spawn with unbounded lifetime needs approval.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Compile times and runtime correctness regress quickly; human-in-loop checkpoint required.
